Import tensorflow as tf, keras from tensorflow, keras_cv, tensorflow_datasets as tfds, and tqdm for progress visualization
Set batch size to 4 and bounding box format to 'xywh'

Define a function unpackage_raw_tfds_inputs that converts Pascal VOC 2007 raw dataset inputs into a dictionary with keys 'images' and 'bounding_boxes' in 'xywh' format, casting classes and boxes to tf.float32

Define a function load_pascal_voc that loads the 'voc/2007' dataset split ('train' or 'test') using tfds.load, maps unpackage_raw_tfds_inputs over the dataset with AUTOTUNE parallel calls, and returns the dataset
Load the training dataset with split='train' and bounding_box_format='xywh' using load_pascal_voc, and similarly load the evaluation dataset with split='test' and bounding_box_format='xywh'
Shuffle the training dataset with a buffer size of batch_size * 4 (i.e., 16)
Apply ragged batching to both train_ds and eval_ds with batch size 4 and drop_remainder=True using ragged_batch()

Create a keras.Sequential augmenter containing keras_cv.layers.RandomFlip with mode='horizontal' and bounding_box_format='xywh', followed by keras_cv.layers.JitteredResize with target_size=(640, 640), scale_factor=(0.75, 1.3), and bounding_box_format='xywh'
Map the augmenter over train_ds with num_parallel_calls=tf.data.AUTOTUNE
Create an inference resizing layer with keras_cv.layers.Resizing(640, 640, pad_to_aspect_ratio=True, bounding_box_format='xywh')
Map the inference resizing layer over eval_ds with num_parallel_calls=tf.data.AUTOTUNE
Define a dict_to_tuple function that converts input dictionaries with keys 'images' and 'bounding_boxes' into tuples (images, bounding_box.to_dense(...)) with max_boxes=32
Map dict_to_tuple over train_ds and eval_ds with num_parallel_calls=tf.data.AUTOTUNE
Prefetch train_ds and eval_ds with tf.data.AUTOTUNE
Create an SGD optimizer with learning_rate=0.005, momentum=0.9, and global_clipnorm=10.0
Initialize a keras_cv.models.RetinaNet model from preset 'resnet50_imagenet' with num_classes matching the Pascal VOC classes (20 classes), and bounding_box_format='xywh'
Compile the model with classification_loss='focal', box_loss='smoothl1', optimizer as above, and metrics=None

Define a custom keras.callbacks.Callback called EvaluateCOCOMetricsCallback that on_epoch_end resets a keras_cv.metrics.BoxCOCOMetrics metric (with bounding_box_format='xywh' and evaluate_freq=1e9), then iterates over the eval_ds in batches, runs model.predict on images, updates metric with ground truth and predictions, calls metric.result(force=True), and updates logs with the metrics
Run model.fit() on train_ds.take(20) with validation_data=eval_ds.take(20), epochs=1, and callbacks containing EvaluateCOCOMetricsCallback(eval_ds.take(20))

Observe that after completion of the first epoch's training loop (around 1271 steps), during the EvaluateCOCOMetricsCallback on_epoch_end execution, an UnknownError is thrown related to tf.concat dimension mismatch with error message indicating shapes [4,2,4] vs [4,1,4] cannot be concatenated along axis 1
Verify error traceback points to keras_cv.metrics.BoxCOCOMetrics in box_coco_metrics.py within _box_concat function using tf.concat on a list of boxes with incompatible shapes

Confirm the error reproducibly occurs in Colab Pro environment after first epoch of training with batch size 4 and ragged batching on Pascal VOC 2007 dataset with KerasCV RetinaNet model from 'resnet50_imagenet' preset
